elements.text: B4-2.3-03, Legal Requirements for Co-op Projects (11/06/2024)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on legal requirements for co-op projects, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amendments to Documents
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Membership
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lien Position for Co-op Share Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Prior Co-op Financing
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Assignment of Co-op's Lease/Occupancy Rights
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Corporation's Recognition Agreement, Responsibilities, and Lender's Rights
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender's Rights
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Amendments to Documents
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The co-op project's documents must provide for the tenant-stockholders to have the right to amend them. In addition, the co-op corporation must be legally bound to notify the holder of a co-op share loan about any proposed material changes to the co-op project with respect to allocation of membership interests, voting rights, insurance coverages, and any other provisions that are for the express benefit of the lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Co-op Membership
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The project documents must require that the sale or transfer of stock, shares, or membership certificates in the co-op corporation be in compliance with federal and state security disclosure laws. The documents also must require tenant-stockholders to own stock, shares, or a membership certificate, and permit the stock, shares, or membership certificates in the co-op corporation to be pledged and registered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project documents must give the tenant-stockholder a right to occupy the unit for a period that extends at least to the maturity date of the share loan, although this right should be subject to the terms and conditions of a proprietary lease or occupancy agreement between the tenant-stockholder and the co-op corporation. The documents also must prohibit the co-op corporation from imposing unreasonable limitations on the tenantstockholder's ability to sell, transfer, or convey their membership, or to sublease their unit. If the purchaser's right to membership or occupancy is subject to any right of the co-op corporation to give approval, the lender must furnish evidence to clearly show that such approval has been given before Fannie Mae will purchase or securitize the co-op share loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Lien Position for Co-op Share Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The share loan must be secured by the assignment (in pledge or trust) of the borrower's leasehold estate; a pledge or trust of the corporation stock, shares, or membership certificate; and any other documents that are appropriate under individual state or local laws and practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender that is financing the share loan must receive an assignment of the proprietary lease, occupancy agreement, or other similar evidence of the right to occupy the unit for all share loans that it delivers to Fannie Mae. The lender must also obtain a stock power, assignment, or other similar document that authorizes the lender to transfer ownership interest in the event of a default. Valid financing statements and assignments of financing statements must be executed and filed, if necessary to perfect Fannie Mae's security interest under the Uniform Commercial Code of the state in which the property is located. Information searches or equivalent evidence of filing financing statements and assignments of financing statements must be obtained and must show that the Fannie Mae co-op share loan is in first-lien position. In those states in which co-op units are considered real property, perfection of the lien must comply with state law applicable to real estate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The share loan must be a first-lien, except that, where custom dictates to the contrary, Fannie Mae will permit its lien to be subordinate to the co-op corporation's lien against the tenant-stockholder's shares for unpaid assessments that represents the pro rata share of the corporation's payments for the blanket mortgage, current year's real estate taxes, operating expenses or maintenance fees, and special assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The pro rata share of the project debt that is related to the co-op share loan cannot exceed 35% of the sum of the related pro rata share of the project debt and the appraised equity interest value of the shares. Lenders may use a higher ratio (not to exceed 40%) when there are fully documented compensating factors that justify using the higher ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will also permit its lien to be subordinate to any assignment of rents or maintenance expenses in any mortgage or deed of trust that is secured by the co-op project, or any Regulatory Agreement entered into by the co-op corporation and the Secretary of HUD as a condition for obtaining HUD mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior Co-op Financing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The co-op project must be in compliance with the requirements imposed by the holder of any prior financing for the project. If the blanket mortgage on a project includes a due-on-encumbrance clause and the project is located in a state in which share loans are considered to be an encumbrance on the project, the blanket lender must consent to the share loan financing. In the case of a conversion of an existing building, the blanket lender must agree to the use of the building as a co-op and, if it is feasible, agree-in the event of a default on the blanket mortgage-not to wipe out the shares of those tenant-stockholders who are current in the payment of their assessments or carrying charges.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Assignment of Co-op's Lease/Occupancy Rights
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Generally, the project documents should not permit the co-op corporation to restrict the sale, conveyance, or transfer of a unit owned by a lender, its successors, or assigns, nor to place any limits on the assignment of the proprietary lease or occupancy agreement to the lender, its successors, or assigns. This lease or agreement must be assumable by the lender if the tenant-stockholder defaults on the share loan. If the co-op's organizational documents require that a tenant-stockholder be a natural person, they must permit the lender to select a non-corporate designee for any assignment of a proprietary lease or occupancy agreement that it acquires through foreclosure or acceptance of a deed in lieu of foreclosure. If the lender assumes the lease or agreement as the result of the tenant-stockholder's default, the co-op corporation must allow the lender to attempt to sell its interest in the lease or agreement. However, if the lender is unable to effect a satisfactory sale within 60 days-either through its own efforts or with assistance from the co-op corporation-the co-op corporation may not prohibit the lender from subletting the unit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project documents may grant the co-op corporation the right to approve a lender's sublessee or to offer an alternate sublessee that is satisfactory to the lender. However, the co-op corporation's approval standards and procedures may not be unreasonably restrictive or in violation of applicable law, and the action must be completed within a reasonable time after the lender requests approval of a proposed sublessee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Co-op Corporation's Recognition Agreement, Responsibilities, and Lender's Rights
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The project documents must either require the co-op corporation to execute a separate agreement-such as a recognition agreement-or include provisions to recognize specific rights of the lender that finances the share loan (or those of its successors or assigns) and the co-op corporation's responsibilities to that lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Co-op Corporation's Responsibilities
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The recognition agreement or other legal agreement (or the project's legal documents) must include, among other things, the following responsibilities for the co-op corporation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The co-op corporation must evict a tenant-stockholder who has defaulted on their share loan and must terminate that tenant-stockholder's lease, if the share loan holder requests it to do so.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The co-op corporation must be legally bound to notify the lender of any of the following changes or occurrences:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any threatened or actual condemnation, eminent domain proceeding or acquisition, or any actual loss, whether or not covered by insurance, that affects any portion of the co-op project or unit;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: failure to maintain compliance with co-operative corporation eligibility under IRS Code Section 216;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any 30-day delinquency by the co-op corporation in payments due under any blanket mortgage for real estate taxes, assessments, and charges imposed by a government entity or public utility, or under any ground lease;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any lapse or cancellation of any insurance coverages maintained by the co-op project;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any proposed action that requires the consent of a specified percentage of eligible share loan holders; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: any 90-day delinquency by the tenant-stockholder that is related to the payment of their monthly assessments or carrying charges.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the co-op project is subject to a ground lease, in the event of a condemnation or similar taking proceeding affecting any portion of the co-op project or the unit, the co-op corporation must protect the lender's financial interest.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender's Rights
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The project documents must grant the lender financing a share loan the right to cure the tenant-stockholder's defaults in their assessment payments or carrying charges and the right to review and approve the following actions before the co-op corporation can consent to them:
elements.label: text

elements.text: any surrender, cancellation, modification, or assignment of any documents evidencing ownership, possession, and use of a unit;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any sublease of a unit;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any further or additional pledge or mortgage of any documents evidencing ownership, possession, and use of a unit;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any action to change the form of ownership of the project; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the contraction, expansion, or termination of the co-op project.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Recent Related Announcements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The table below provides references to recently issued Announcement that are related to this topic.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Announcements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Issue Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Announcement SEL-2024-07
elements.label: text

elements.text: November 06, 2024
elements.label: text

elements.text: B4-2.3-04, Loan Eligibility for Co-op Share Loans (08/06/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on loan eligibility for co-op share loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loan Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Calculating the LTV Ratio for Co-op Share Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loans Subject to Flip Tax
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loan Documentation
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Whole Loan and MBS Delivery Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Co-op share loans finance the purchase or refinancing of the borrower's ownership interest in a co-op housing corporation and accompanying occupancy rights in a residential unit in a co-op project owned by the co-op housing corporation. The property that secures Fannie Mae's first lien is the borrower's ownership interest in a co-op housing corporation that is represented by stock or shares in the co-op housing corporation (or by a membership certificate or other contractual agreement evidencing ownership) and an assignment of the borrower's rights under a proprietary lease or occupancy agreement with the co-op housing corporation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Co-op Share Loan Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase co-op share loans provided borrowers occupy the property as a principal residence or second home. Investment properties are prohibited.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the applicable credit score, minimum reserve requirements, and maximum debt-to-income ratio requirements, see the Eligibility Matrix.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculating the LTV Ratio for Co-op Share Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The method for calculating the LTV ratio for a co-op share loan is based on whether the borrower assumes their pro rata share of the blanket mortgage or does not. In those markets where the borrower assumes their pro rata share of the blanket mortgage, the LTV ratio is determined by dividing the original loan amount by the lower of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sales price for the co-op unit (unencumbered by the unit's pro rata share of the co-op project's blanket mortgage(s)), or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the appraised value of the co-op stock or shares and the related occupancy rights (unencumbered by the unit's pro rata share of the project's blanket mortgage(s)).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: In those markets where the borrower does not assume their pro rata share of the blanket mortgage, then the LTV ratio is determined by dividing the original loan amount by the lower of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sales price for the co-op unit, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the appraised value of the co-op stock or shares and the related occupancy rights.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loans Subject to Flip Tax
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Co-op share loans secured by units in co-op projects that require the payment of a 'flip tax' are eligible for delivery as long as the co-op project's legal documents permit the imposition of a flip tax and provide for one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lender is exempt from paying the flip tax if the lender acquires the co-op unit in foreclosure, in a transfer by the borrower in lieu of foreclosure, or any other transfer of the borrower's interest in the co-op unit in full or partial satisfaction of the borrower's obligations under the co-op share loan; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the flip tax is payable when the sales price of the co-op unit exceeds the existing unit owner's purchase price (based on property appreciation) and then is assessed only on the amount of the appreciation in
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: value (this flip tax is profit-based).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the flip tax does not meet one of these requirements and is due whether or not the sales price exceeds the existing unit owner's purchase price, then it may still be eligible as long as the amount of the flip tax is less than or equal to 5% of the value of the property (calculated as the lesser of appraised value or sales price) and it is calculated in one of the following ways:
elements.label: text

elements.text: a flat fee,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a fee per share,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a percentage of the appraised value or sales price of the co-op unit, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a dollar amount per room.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loan Documentation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae does not publish multistate standard co-op share loan instruments because of the variations in state laws pertaining to the co-op form of ownership. If a lender elects to use the Fannie Mae fixed-rate note forms for co-op share loans, the lender represents and warrants that the notes comply with all applicable laws and regulations for co-op share loans in and are enforceable and negotiable under the laws of the applicable jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae publishes state-specific documentation requirements for states in which Fannie Mae purchases coop share loans on Fannie Mae's website. Those requirements describe documents that must be delivered to the document custodian (for example, co-op Recognition Agreement, assignments to Fannie Mae, and evidence of share ownership) and documents that the lender must retain in the individual loan file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whole Loan and MBS Delivery Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Co-op share loans may be delivered as whole loans in standard commitments. Co-op share loans pooled in MBS may be eligible for delivery as long as they meet the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: C3-5-05, Commingling ARMs in MBS; and

elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter B5-1, High-Balance Mortgage Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes the policies and requirements related to high-balance mortgage loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B5-1-01, High-Balance Mortgage Loan Eligibility and Underwriting (07/02/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains loan eligibility and underwriting information on high-balance mortgage loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Limits
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Eligibility and Underwriting Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: High LTV Refinance Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Government Mortgage Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Limits
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The high-balance loan requirements apply to mortgage loans with original loan amounts meeting the high-cost area loan limits established by the Federal Housing Finance Agency. Fannie Mae publishes on its website the maximum high-cost area loan limits that may apply by state (or territory); however, specific loan limits are established for each county (or equivalent) and may be lower for each specific high-cost area. Refer to Loan Limits for Conventional Mortgages for additional information, including the loan limits for each area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders are responsible for ensuring that the original principal balance of each mortgage loan does not exceed the applicable maximum loan limit for the specific area in which the property is located. To assist lenders in determining the applicable limits, Fannie Mae posts reference material on its website, including the Loan Limit Geocoder, which lenders can use to look up loan limits based on a specific address (or batch of addresses).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Loan Eligibility and Underwriting Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: High-balance mortgage loans must meet all standard Fannie Mae eligibility and underwriting requirements, as outlined in this Selling Guide , except as noted in this section. The following guidelines apply to all high-balance mortgage loans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans must be conventional first-lien mortgages only.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loans must meet the LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios as outlined in the Eligibility Matrix .
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All loans must be underwritten through DU.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For additional eligibility information, see the Eligibility Matrix. For information about loan delivery, see B5-1-02, High-Balance Pricing, Mortgage Insurance, Special Feature Codes, and Delivery Limitations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : Unless otherwise notified by Fannie Mae, existing variances in the Lender Contract apply to high-balance mortgage loans; however, the more restrictive of the eligibility requirements of this section or the lender's variance will apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: High LTV Refinance Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: High-balance mortgage loans are eligible for high LTV refinance options. The eligibility and appraisal requirements specific to high LTV refinance loans supersede all requirements that apply to high-balance mortgage loans. See B5-7-01, High LTV Refinance Loan and Borrower Eligibility for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Government Mortgage Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For details, see B6-1-01, General Government Mortgage Loan Requirements, B6-1-02, Eligible FHA-Insured Mortgage Loans, B6-1-03, Eligible VA-Guaranteed Mortgages, and B6-1-05, Eligible RD-Guaranteed Mortgages.

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information about the following aspects of high-balance mortgage loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pricing/Loan-Level Price Adjustments
elements.label: list_item

elements.label: list_item

elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Pricing/Loan-Level Price Adjustments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Live pricing options are provided for high-balance mortgage loan transactions in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application. Specific additional LLPAs apply to all high-balance mortgage loans, whether delivered under whole loan commitments or MBS contracts. High-balance mortgage loans are also subject to all other applicable LLPAs. All price adjustments are cumulative. For details, see the Loan-Level Price Adjustment (LLPA) Matrix.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Insurance Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgage insurance coverage is required for high-balance mortgage loans with LTV ratios greater than 80%. Financed borrower-purchased mortgage insurance is permitted; however, the maximum gross LTV (after the inclusion of the financed premium) cannot exceed 95%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Data Requirements Including Special Feature Codes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender must report SFC 808 when delivering a high-balance loan to Fannie Mae, except for government loans and unless otherwise instructed. All other applicable SFCs must also be reported as required for the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: High-Balance Whole Loan and MBS Delivery Limitations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae's requirements regarding delivery limitations for nonstandard loans apply to high-balance mortgage loans. For details see C2-2-01, General Requirements for Good Delivery of Whole Loans, and C3-2-01, Determining Eligibility for Loans Pooled into MBS. Furthermore, lenders may deliver high-balance mortgage loans into a Fannie Majors TBA-eligible pool. For details, see C3-6-01, Parameters for Pooling Loans Into Fannie Majors.

elements.text: Chapter B5-2, Manufactured Housing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes the requirements for originating and underwriting mortgage loans secured by manufactured housing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B5-2-01, Manufactured Housing (11/06/2024)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on manufactured housing, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Housing Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Variances
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Indemnification
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Manufactured Housing Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Any dwelling unit built on a permanent chassis that is attached to a permanent foundation system and evidenced by a HUD Data Plate or HUD Certification Label(s) (for each section of the home) is a manufactured home for purposes of Fannie Mae's guidelines. This includes properties meeting the MH Advantage requirements described in B2-3-02, Special Property Eligibility and Underwriting Considerations: Factory-Built Housing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The manufactured home and the land on which it is situated must be legally classified as real property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other factory-built housing -such as modular, prefabricated, panelized, or sectional housing-is not considered manufactured housing and mortgage loans secured by such housing are eligible under the guidelines stated in Subpart B2, Eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Government insured or guaranteed manufactured housing loans are not subject to conventional guidelines for manufactured housing and therefore are subject to the provisions set forth in Chapter B6-1, Government Insured and Guaranteed Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders are not required to obtain specific approval to deliver loans secured by manufactured homes. However, in some cases, lenders must obtain Fannie Mae's project acceptance for condo or PUD projects consisting of manufactured homes. See  B4-2.2-06, Project Eligibility Review Service (PERS) for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Variances
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Unless specifically stated in the terms of the contract, variances or other terms contained in any lender's contract are not eligible for use with mortgages secured by manufactured homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Indemnification
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders are subject to all indemnification obligations as described in A2-1-03, Indemnification for Losses.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on manufactured housing loan eligibility, including:

elements.text: General Loan Eligibility Criteria

elements.text: General Loan Eligibility Criteria
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae purchases loans secured by manufactured homes that meet the following general criteria:
elements.label: text

elements.text: first-lien mortgages only,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: fully amortizing fixed-rate loans,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: fully amortizing ARM loans with initial fixed-rate periods of 7 years or 10 years,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: principal residences (single- and multi-width), and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: second home dwellings (multi-width only).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Refer to the Eligibility Matrix for additional restrictions and maximum allowable LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A manufactured home may be located on an individual lot or in a condo or PUD project development, see Project Review Methods in  B4-2.1-01, General Information on Project Standards  for additional information. A manufactured home located on leased land and subject to a ground lease is only eligible if the manufactured home is owned (not leased) by the borrower and in a condo or PUD project approved by Fannie Mae's Project Eligibility Review Service (PERS), see B4-2.2-06, Project Eligibility Review Service (PERS).
elements.label: text

elements.text: See  B5-5.3-03, Shared Equity Transactions: Eligibility, Underwriting and Collateral Requirements when a manufactured home is subject to a community land trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ineligible Manufactured Housing Criteria
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following are ineligible for loans secured by manufactured housing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: investment properties,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: manufactured homes located on leased land and subject to a ground lease, unless located in a Fannie Mae-approved condo or PUD project, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: manufactured homes in co-op projects.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Manufactured Housing Standards
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The loan must be secured by both the manufactured home and the borrower's interest in the land on which it is situated, and both the manufactured home and the land must be legally classified as real property under applicable state law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See B2-3-02, Special Property Eligibility and Underwriting Considerations: Factory-Built Housing,  and the Manufactured Housing Product Matrix for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) 3.6 Policy
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Lenders using UAD 3.6 must follow the requirements in the UAD 3.6 Policy Supplement .

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on manufactured housing underwriting considerations, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwriting and DU Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Amount
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Down Payment Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Traded Manufactured Homes

elements.text: Construction-to-Permanent Transactions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) 3.6 Supplement
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Underwriting and DU Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Loans secured by manufactured homes (including MH Advantage properties) must be underwritten through DU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: When entering the property information into DU, the lender must correctly identify the property type as a manufactured home or MH Advantage, and identify whether it is in a condo, co-op, or PUD project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: DU does not distinguish between single-width and multi-width.
elements.label: text

elements.text: DU checks the subject property addresses against manufactured home property addresses in the DU property database. If DU's database indicates the property may be a manufactured home, DU will return a message alerting the lender. DU's issuance of this message does not necessarily mean the property is a manufactured home, nor does the absence of this message indicate that Fannie Mae accepts the accuracy of the property type as it was submitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must research the subject property type. If it is determined the property is a manufactured home, the lender must correct the property type and resubmit the loan casefile to DU. If it is NOT a manufactured home, the loan may be delivered with the appraisal recommendation provided by DU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: DU will issue a message reminding the lender to ensure that the MH Advantage or single-width manufactured home requirements are met, in addition to all other manufactured housing requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Amount
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The loan amount may include the following costs:
elements.label: text

elements.text: cost of the manufactured home;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: cost of the land;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the costs of construction, including
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: bona fide and documented transportation costs,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: costs for site preparation, which may include the cost to remove an existing manufactured home and other outbuildings,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: foundation,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: establishing utilities,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: all site improvements, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: dwelling installation at the site.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Any personal property items (non-realty items) purchased in conjunction with the manufactured home must be deducted from the sales price and cannot be financed as part of the loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Down Payment Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A minimum down payment of 5% must come from the borrower's own funds unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the LTV or CLTV ratio is less than or equal to 80%;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the borrower is purchasing a one-unit principal residence and meets the requirements to use gifts, donated grant funds, or funds received from an employer to pay for some or all of the borrower's minimum contribution. See B3-4.3-04, Personal Gifts; B3-4.3-06, Grants and Lender Contributions; and B3-4.3-08, Employer Assistance, for additional information; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the property meets the MH Advantage requirements and the loan meets the requirements for LTV ratios of 95.01-97%. In this case, the borrower must contribute a minimum down payment of 3%, from their
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: own funds unless the loan meets the gift, grant, or funds from an employer policy referenced above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The borrower's equity in the land is considered the borrower's own funds. Where the borrower holds title to the land on which the manufactured home will be permanently attached, the value of the land may be credited toward the borrower's minimum down payment (or equity requirement for a refinance). The borrower's equity contribution will be the difference between any outstanding liens against the land and the market value of the land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table describes how to determine the value of the land based on when and how the borrower acquired the land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of Land Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Value of the Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: More than 12 months preceding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the loan application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current appraised value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: None.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12 or fewer months preceding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the date of the loan application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lesser of the sales price or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the current appraised value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender must document the
elements.label: text

elements.text: borrower's cash investment by
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtaining:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a copy of the settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a copy of the warranty deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: that shows there are no
elements.label: text

elements.text: outstanding liens against the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a copy of the release of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior liens(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The borrower acquired the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: at any time as a gift, inheritance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or other non-purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current appraised value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate documentation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: verify the acquisition and
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of ownership of the land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trade Equity from the Borrower's Existing Manufactured Home
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Trade equity from the borrower's existing manufactured home may be used as part of the borrower's minimum down payment requirement. The maximum equity contribution from the traded manufactured home is 90% of the retail value for the traded manufactured home based on the NADA Manufactured Housing Appraisal Guide except:
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the borrower has owned the traded manufactured home for less than 12 months preceding the date of the loan application, the maximum equity contribution is the lesser of 90% of the retail value or the lowest price at which the home was sold during that 12 month period.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Any costs associated with the removal of the traded home or any outstanding indebtedness secured by liens on the home must be deducted from the maximum equity contribution.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Traded Manufactured Homes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For traded manufactured homes, Fannie Mae requires a lien search in the appropriate real property and personal property records to verify ownership and to determine whether there are any existing liens on the manufactured home and land, or on the home and the land if they are encumbered by separate liens. The seller of the new manufactured home must provide proof of title transfer and satisfaction of any existing liens on the traded manufactured home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Money Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Purchase money transactions are those in which the mortgage proceeds are used to finance the purchase of the manufactured home or the manufactured home and the land. The land may be previously owned by the borrower, either free of any mortgage or subject to a mortgage that will be paid off with the proceeds of the new purchase money mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The borrower does not receive any cash back with a purchase money transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: New Manufactured Homes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The LTV ratio (and CLTV/HCLTV ratio, if applicable) for a loan secured by a newly built manufactured home that is being attached to a permanent foundation system in connection with a purchase transaction will be based on the lower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sales price of the manufactured home plus:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lowest sales price at which the land was sold during that 12 month period if the land was purchased in the 12 months preceding the loan application date; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the current appraised value of the land if the land was purchased more than 12 months preceding the loan application date.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the 'as completed' appraised value of the manufactured home and land.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Existing Manufactured Homes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: An existing manufactured home is one that already exists on its foundation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home Subdivision Development
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: In cases where a manufactured home is being sold to a consumer by a builder, developer, or manufacturer acting as a developer as part of a new or existing manufactured home subdivision, the LTV ratio (and CLTV/HCLTV ratio, if applicable) for a loan secured by an existing manufactured home will be based on the lower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sales price of the manufactured home and land; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the current appraised value of the manufactured home and land.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All Other Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The LTV ratio (and CLTV/HCLTV ratio, if applicable) for a loan secured by an existing manufactured home will be based on the lowest of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sales price of the manufactured home and land;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the current appraised value of the manufactured home and land; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: if the manufactured home was built in the 12 months preceding the loan application date, the lowest price at which the home was previously sold during that 12-month period, plus the lower of:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the current appraised value of the land, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lowest price at which the land was sold during that 12-month period (if there was such a sale).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note: The above purchase requirements do not apply to single-closing construction-to-permanent transactions. See B5-3.1-02, Conversion of Construction-to-Permanent Financing: Single-Closing Transactions for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Limited Cash-Out Refinance Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Limited cash-out refinance transactions may involve the following scenarios:
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff of an existing personal property lien on a new manufactured home (or an existing lien on the home and a mortgage on the land if encumbered by separate liens), or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: payoff of a first lien mortgage secured by an existing manufactured home and land (or existing mortgages for the home and land if encumbered by separate liens).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The maximum LTV ratio (and CLTV ratio, if applicable) for a limited cash-out refinance transaction for a loan secured by a manufactured home and land will be based on the lower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the current appraised value of the manufactured home and land; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: if the manufactured home was owned by the borrower for less than 12 months on the loan application date and:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: if the home and land are secured by separate liens, the lowest price at which the home was previously sold during that 12-month period plus the lower of the current appraised value of the land, or the lowest sales price at which the land was sold during that 12-month period (if there was such a sale);
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: if the home and land are secured by a single lien, the lowest price at which the home and land were previously sold during that 12-month period.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Proceeds of a limited cash-out refinance mortgage may be used to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay off the outstanding principal balance of an existing personal property lien or first lien mortgage secured by the manufactured home and land (or existing liens if the home and land were encumbered by separate first liens);
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: pay off the outstanding principal balance of an existing subordinate mortgage or lien secured by the manufactured home and/or land, but only if it was used to purchase the manufactured home and/or land;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: finance costs of construction;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: finance closing costs (including prepaid expenses); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: provide cash back to the borrower in an amount that does not exceed the greater of 1% of the balance of the new refinance mortgage or $2,000.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Cash-Out Refinance Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A cash-out refinance:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: involves the payoff of an existing first lien mortgage secured by the manufactured home and land (or existing liens if the home and land were encumbered by separate first liens); or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: enables the property owner to obtain a mortgage on a property that does not already have a mortgage lien against it, and permits the borrower to take equity out of the property in the form of mortgage proceeds that may be used for any purpose.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: To be eligible for a cash-out refinance, the property must be a multi-width manufactured home (single-width are not permitted). The borrower must have owned both the manufactured home and land for at least 12 months preceding the date of the loan application. The LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios will be based on the current appraised value of the manufactured home and land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: New Construction of a Manufactured Home
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When the mortgage loan funds the construction of a new manufactured home, construction must be complete when the loan is purchased (or securitized) by Fannie Mae. As a reminder, if construction is completed after the first payment date of the subject loan, the loan may be subject to the property value requirements (loans more than four months old at time of purchase) or seasoned loan requirements in  B2-1.5-02, Loan Eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction-to-Permanent Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following construction-to-permanent transactions are permitted for the construction and permanent financing of a manufactured home:
elements.label: text

elements.text: single-closing transactions processed as a purchase or limited cash-out refinance, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: two-closing limited cash-out refinances. (Two-closing cash-out refinances are not permitted.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan must meet the requirements in Section B5-3.1, Conversion of Construction-to-Permanent Financing, and all other manufactured home requirements in this Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) 3.6 Supplement
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders using UAD 3.6 must follow the requirements in the UAD 3.6 Policy Supplement.
